core_mechanism: continuous parametric deformation of a system's eigenvalue dispersion relation by an externally applied transverse control parameter, driving qualitative (bifurcation-like) reorganizations of the spectrum: closing of spectral gaps, sign reversal of the dispersion's slope and curvature, and topological restructuring of the set of extremal (Fermi-level) points
brief_reason: The abstract states that an externally applied transverse voltage continuously and reversibly reshapes the bands so that the Fermi surface fractures, the Fermi velocity and effective mass change sign, and excitation gaps shrink, which is exactly a tunable transverse perturbation qualitatively deforming an eigenvalue dispersion relation.
